NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF1 TORNADO SOUTH OF HOLLANSBURG IN SOUTHWEST DARKE COUNTY. ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 90 MPH, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS AND PATH LENGTH OF 1.5 MILES

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF1 TORNADO IN THE VILLAGE OF NEW MADISON IN SOUTHWEST DARKE COUNTY. MUCH OF THE DAMAGE WAS EF0, BUT BARN ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE WAS MORE CONDUCIVE TO EF1. ESTIMATED PATH WIDTH 200 YARDS AND ESTIMATED PATH LENGTH OF 0.4 MILES

*** 1 FATAL *** AN EF3 TORNADO WAS CONFIRMED IN AND JUST WEST OF CELINA IN MERCER COUNTY OHIO. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE INCLUDED 2 HOMES WITH COMPLETE ROOF REMOVAL AND MANY EXTERIOR WALLS COLLAPSED. ANOTHER HOME WAS LIFTED COMPLETELY OFF ITS FOUNDATION AND DROPPED 70 YARDS AWAY IN A FIELD. MANY OTHER HOUSES... VEHICLES... AND TREES WERE DAMAGED ALONG THE PATH.
BUILDING AT OHIO 121 AND 72 WITH DOOR BLOWN OFF. DAMAGE POSSIBLY CAUSED BY A TORNADO.
TREE DOWN ON HOUSE. MANY POWER LINES AND TREES DOWN OVER ROADS IN AREA. DAMAGE POSSIBLY CAUSED BY A TORNADO.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN, POSSIBLY CAUSED BY A TORNADO.
EF2 TORNADO CONFIRMED NEAR WEST MILTON, AFFECTING DARKE AND MIAMI COUNTIES IN OHIO. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO HOMES AND TREES.
EF3 TORNADO CONFIRMED NEAR WEST MILTON, AFFECTING DARKE AND MIAMI COUNTIES IN OHIO. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO HOMES AND TREES. THIS TORNADO RATING WAS CHANGED FROM EF2 TO EF3.

*** 1 INJ *** AN EF1 TORNADO WAS CONFIRMED WEST OF WAPAKONETA IN AUGLAIZE COUNTY OHIO. AN ENTIRE ROOM OF ONE HOME WAS PULLED FROM THE STRUCTURE AND DESTROYED. AT LEAST A HALF DOZEN BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE ALSO DESTROYED.

CORRECTED FOR UPGRADE TO EF4. NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS EF4 TORNADO WITH DAMAGE ACROSS MUCH OF MONTGOMERY COUNTY. TOTAL INJURIES UNKNOWN. CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE TO HOMES, BUSINESSES AND TREES. ESTIMATED MAX WIND SPEED IS 170 MPH, WIDTH OF 0.6 MILES, AND LENGTH OF 19 MILES.

A SPOTTER NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF TURNER ROAD AND KLEPINGER ROAD MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 89 MPH ON THEIR HOME WEATHER STATION BEFORE THE STATION FAILED. LIKELY ASSOCIATED WITH THE TORNADO NEAR TROTWOOD IN MONTGOMERY COUNTY.
